hub_subtitle: Business English para as reuniões com o time Global da Sanofi, congressos médicos e a apresentação de agosto

lesson.menu_title: Midpoint Milestone -- Progress Review: Looking Back at 16 Weeks
lesson.menu_desc: Meio do caminho: revisar o que ela ja domina e ela mesma descrever, em voz alta, seu papel na Sanofi e seus resultados. Reune tudo -- past simple, present simple, present continuous, going to e comparativos -- e o vocabulario consolidado (results, challenge, launch, target, benchmark, growth, gap). Nova expressao: 'Looking back at...' -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 17 -- Midpoint Milestone: Progress Review (Looking Back at...)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Emmanuele Orrico | Lesson 17 -- Midpoint Milestone: Progress Review

listenings.text: Hi Emmanuele, it's David. Just a quick message before our review call. Looking back at the last four months, I want to say thank you. In our first meetings, you translated every sentence in your head. Now you present the Brazil results in English, live. Your growth is very clear to the Global team. This year, Brazil is stronger than last year, and you talk about it with confidence. Our biggest challenge for the second half is the August meeting, so we will focus on that. See you on the call.

Hi Emmanuele, it's Claire from the Global team. A quick recap of your first sixteen classes before your review. First, you learned to introduce yourself and your role. Then you learned the past, the present, and your future plans. Last month you compared Brazil's results against the global benchmark, and you did it very well. Looking back at all of this, you are not the same speaker you were in January. In the second half, we are going to work on speed and on the launch presentation. You are doing great. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: Looking back at the first half, I am proud of my progress. In January, I translated every sentence in my head. Now I present the Brazil results in English. Our growth this year is stronger than last year. My biggest challenge is speed, and I am going to work on it.

items.situation: The teacher asks: "Describe your role at Sanofi in one sentence."
tips: I'm the National Demand Manager at Sanofi.
I'm responsible for eight disease areas in immunology.

items.situation: "What are you working on right now?"
tips: I'm working on the Rain Plan for the second half.
Right now, I'm preparing the results for the August meeting.

items.situation: "How are this year's results, compared to last year?"
tips: Our growth is stronger than last year.
This year, our results are better than last year.

items.situation: "What was your biggest challenge last quarter?"
tips: Our biggest challenge was market access.
Last quarter, the biggest challenge was the launch.

items.situation: "What are you going to focus on in the second half?"
tips: I'm going to focus on speed and confidence.
I'm going to work on the launch presentation.

items.situation: "Looking back at the first half, what are you proud of?"
tips: Looking back at January, I'm proud of my progress.
Looking back at the first classes, I don't translate every sentence now.
